title: Alaska again leads nation in TB infection rates
summary: Though widely thought of as a disease of the past, tuberculosis, commonly referred to as TB, is still a problem in Alaska. For the third year in a row, the Last Frontier has come in first among U.S. states for its rate of new infections. Rates of new infection are highest in rural Alaska, and Alaska Natives are most at risk for contracting the disease. New drug-resistant strains may play a factor in the general resurgence of TB in other parts of the world since the 1980s, but in Alaska, the illness's legacy is a big contributor to its present. According to the Alaska Department of Health and Social Services, in 2013, Alaska had a new-case TB infection rate of 9.6 per 100,000 people.
